revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
[[ja:愛宕神社 (亀岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Atago-jinja (Kameoka city) honden.JPG |name = Atago Shrine |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|Kameoka, Kyoto|en|Kameoka, Kyoto|qid=Q633996|lt=Kameoka, Kyoto|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Chitose-chō Kokubu Minami Yamanoro 1 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.04105555555556|N|135.58694444444444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|de|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|zh|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}
{{ill|Izanami|de|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|zh|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|de|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|zh|大国主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|zh|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|de|Dorfschrein (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|zh|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |established = (trad.) reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} 1 (507 ?)
(mythic origins) |main_building = One-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |other_names = Atako Shrine (阿多古神社); Moto-Atago; Hon-miya of Atago Shrines |festival = 21 October |rituals = Fire-prevention Festival (24 April) |map = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Atago Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256|qq=}} |location = {{ill|Kameoka|qid=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} |deity = {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} }} '''Atago Shrine''' (愛宕神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine in Chitose-chō Kokubu, {{ill|Kameoka, Kyoto|en|Kameoka, Kyoto|qid=Q633996|lt=Kameoka, Kyoto|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, Japan. Formerly called “Atako Shrine” (阿多古神社), it is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|de|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|zh|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and held the Rank of village shrine (村社) under the pre-war State Shintō Ranking system. By shrine tradition, the well-known {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|de|Atago-Schrein (Kyoto)|ja|愛宕神社 (京都市)|zh|爱宕神社 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on {{ill|Mount Atago|en|Mount Atago|qid=Q2975204|lt=Mount Atago (Kyoto)|1=Mount Atago}} was founded from this site, hence the epithets “Moto-Atago” and “Hon-miya.” Like its Kyoto counterpart, it is famous for the folk custom “three visits to Atago before the age of three,” believed to grant lifelong protection from fire. ==Deities== * {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|de|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|zh|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} (also {{ill|Kagututi|de|Kagututi|ja|軻遇突智神|zh|火之迦具土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagututi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ|1=Kagu-tsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1640658}}) * {{ill|Izanami|de|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美神|zh|伊邪那美命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|de|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主神|zh|大国主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} ==History== ===Overview=== Shrine lore holds that worship began in the mythic age, the mountain itself serving as a {{ill|Himorogi|en|Himorogi|qid=Q60587|lt=Himorogi|1=Himorogi}}. Buildings were first erected in 507 CE during the reign of Emperor Keitai. The '''''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''''' records court Ranks for the kami “Atago” (愛当護神 / 阿当護神) in 864, 872 and 879 CE. The ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' lists “Atako Jinja” in Kuwata District, Tanba Province—identified with this shrine. With the decline of nearby {{ill|Tanba Kokubun-ji|en|Tanba Kokubun-ji|qid=Q10877355|lt=Tanba Kokubun-ji|1=Tanba Kokubun-ji}}, temple monks served here; under {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} the shrine came to be called {{ill|Atago Gongen|en|Atago Gongen|qid=Q1101132|lt=Atago Gongen|1=Atago Gongen}}. The Buddhist image is now kept at Yōsen-ji to the west. ===Court Rank=== * 10 May 864 (Jōgan 6) – Senior Sixth ⇒ Junior Fifth, Lower (愛当護神) * 29 Nov 872 (Jōgan 14) – Junior Fifth, Lower ⇒ Junior Fifth, Upper (阿当護神) * 24 Int. Oct 879 (Gangyō 3) – Junior Fifth, Upper ⇒ Junior Fourth, Lower (阿当護神) ==Shrine precinct== [[File:Atago-jinja (Kameoka city) zenkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Entire precinct]] The hondō, rebuilt c. 1280 in the late Kamakura period, is an Important Cultural Property. Three notable trees are protected as “Famous Trees of Kameoka”: * {{ill|Podocarpus macrophyllus|en|Podocarpus macrophyllus|qid=Q165063|lt=Podocarpus macrophyllus|1=Podocarpus macrophyllus}} – 19 m H, 2.73 m girth * Giant {{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|qid=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria|1=Cryptomeria}} – 29 m H, 5.07 m girth * {{ill|Abies firma|en|Abies firma|qid=Q1718147|lt=Abies firma|1=Abies firma}} – 32 m H, 3.6 m girth The grove is also one of “Kameoka’s 100 natural sites” for its flying-squirrel habitat. File:Atago-jinja (Kameoka city) torii.JPG|Torii File:Atago-jinja (Kameoka city) sekihi.JPG|Shrine name stone File:Atago-jinja Inumaki.jpg|Podocarpus macrophyllus File:Atago-jinja Oosugi.jpg|Giant cryptomeria ==Subsidiary & branch shrines== [[File:Atago-jinja (Kameoka city) keidaisha (hachimanguu-jinja).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Hachiman-gū Shrine]] * '''Hachiman-gū Shrine''' – deity: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} *: Transferred from {{ill|Usa Jingū|de|Usa-Hachiman-Schrein|ja|宇佐八幡宮|zh|宇佐神宫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}} during 729–749 CE; moved here in 1835. * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|de|Toyouke|ja|豊受比売|zh|丰受大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}} Shrine * Fire-Protection Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} * {{ill|Wakasenu-no-Kami|de|Wakasenu|ja|稚産霊|zh|稚产灵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakasenu-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ワクムスビ|1=Wakumusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10921330}} Shrine * {{ill|Haniyama-hime|de|Haniyama-hime|ja|埴山姫|zh|埴山姬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ハニヤス|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9285919}} Shrine * {{ill|Sukunabikona|de|Sukunabikona|ja|少毘古那|zh|少彦名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Shrine * {{ill|Hiruko|de|Hiruko|ja|蛭子|zh|蛭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1067028}} Shrine * Kōra Tamatare Ōkami – deity: {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|qid=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} * Tenman-gū Shrine * (unknown) – Hagimori Daimyōjin; {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|qid=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}} * (unknown) – {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakuhi|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}; {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|en|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|1=Ōyamatsumi}} ==Atago lanterns== [[File:Lanterns.png|thumb|70px|right|Atago lantern in Kawarahayashi-chō]] Several ‘’Atago lanterns’’ stand in Kameoka. Their portable candlestands circulate from house to house, each family lighting the candle before passing it on.‘Atago Lanterns’, Friends of Kameoka City Cultural Museum, 27 March 1994. ==Major annual observances== * Fire-prevention Festival – 24 April ==Cultural properties== ===Important Cultural Property (national)=== * '''Hondō''' (plus 17 old members & 2 roof ledgers) – designated 28 Jan 1977.{{ill|National cultural properties of Japan|de|Wichtige Kulturgüter Japans|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|zh|日本国指定文化财等数据库|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National cultural properties of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ID 102-1985. ==Access== ;Address {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kameoka, Kyoto|en|Kameoka, Kyoto|qid=Q633996|lt=Kameoka, Kyoto|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}} Chitose-chō Kokubu Minami Yamanoro 1 ;Public transport * From {{ill|Kameoka Station|en|Kameoka Station|qid=Q1043696|lt=Kameoka Station|1=Kameoka Station}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en|Chiyokawa Station|qid=Q1043705|lt=Chiyokawa Station|1=Chiyokawa Station}} (JR West Sagano Line), take the {{ill|Kameoka Furusato Bus|de|Kameoka Furusato Bus|ja|亀岡市ふるさとバス|zh|龟冈市故乡巴士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka Furusato Bus|1=Kameoka City Furusato Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370903}} to “Kokubu,” then walk ≈5 min. ;By car * ≈20 min from {{ill|Kameoka IC|de|Kameoka IC|ja|亀岡インターチェンジ|zh|龟冈交流道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kameoka IC|1=Kameoka interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11181996}} on the {{ill|Kyoto Jūkan Expressway|en|Kyoto Jūkan Expressway|qid=Q11375668|lt=Kyoto Jūkan Expressway|1=Kyoto Jūkan Expressway}}. Free parking available. ==Nearby== * {{ill|Izumo-daijingū|en|Izumo-daijingū|qid=Q10896675|lt=Izumo Daijingū|1=Izumo-daijingū}} – ichinomiya of Tanba Province * Ruins of Tanba Kokubun-ji ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==Bibliography== {{small|Sources used in drafting|qq=}} * On-site explanatory plaque. ==Further reading== {{small|Related literature|qq=}} * {{Cite book|ja|year=1923|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976612/34|qq=}} Atago Shrine, Kokubu]|title=Report on Historic Sites & Scenic Places 5|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Atago-jinja (Kameoka, Kyoto)|qq=}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/360301.html Atako Jinja] – Kokugakuin Univ. Shintō & Shrine DB {{Shinto navbox|de|Shintō-Navleiste|ja|神道 横|zh|神道 横|qq=}} {{Atago Faith|de|Atago-Glaube|ja|Atago Faith|zh|爱宕信仰|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|愛宕神社 (亀岡市)|version=103610442|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:愛宕神社 (亀岡市)]] [[de:Atago-Schrein (Kameoka)]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Atago Shrine (Kameoka) (Q11493857)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Tanba Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kameoka)|lt=Atago Shrine (Kameoka)|ja|愛宕神社 (亀岡市)|qid=Q11493857|1=Atago Shrine (Kameoka)}} || — || — || — || {{ill|Atagono Shrine|lt=Atagono Shrine|qid=Q135040494|1=Atagono Shrine}} || {{coord|35.041056|135.586944|display=inline}} || — |} {{wikidata link|Q11493857}}==Notes== [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]] [[Category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:亀岡市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Son-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Atago shrines]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Architecture of Kamakura period]]
